summary: McKenzie describes creating an optimized landing page for a specific AdWords ad group targeting teachers searching for Dolch sight words. He's tailoring the page as a sales presentation for people already in the mood for classroom bingo activities. He notes with self-aware humor that he's beginning to think like an SEO professional, using descriptive anchor text and seeking quality inbound links.
key_points: Creating targeted landing pages for specific ad groups improves conversion by matching visitor intent
Even when your product isn't exactly what someone searched for, honest ad copy can still capture relevant demand
SEO thinking — descriptive anchor text, quality inbound links — becomes instinctive once you start optimizing for search
money_quotes: I spent a bit of time making basically a sales presentation to a person in the mood for Dolch Sight Word Bingo.
I am starting to think like an SEO person. This is beginning to freak me out.
key_insight: Effective online marketing means creating dedicated landing pages that match the specific intent of each ad group rather than sending all traffic to a generic homepage.
